  <td width="6%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:6.6%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">27</font></p>
  </td>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">PART&nbsp;I</font></b><a name="Parti_233654"></a></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 1.</font></b><a name="Item1__233727"></a><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>BUSINESS</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">General</font></b><a name="General_005629"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Appliance Recycling
Centers of America,&nbsp;Inc. and Subsidiaries (&#147;we,&#148; the &#147;Company&#148; or &#147;ARCA&#148;)
are in the business of selling new major household appliances through a chain
of Company-owned factory outlet stores under the name ApplianceSmart</font><font size="1" style="font-size:6.5pt;position:relative;top:-3.0pt;">&#174;</font>.&#160; We also provide turnkey appliance recycling
services for electric utilities and other sponsors of energy efficiency
programs.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are a leading retailer
and recycler of major household appliances and generate revenues from:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Retail sales of
appliances at our ApplianceSmart<font size="1" style="font-size:6.5pt;position:relative;top:-3.0pt;">&#174;</font>&#160;Factory Outlets.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.6pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Fees
charged for collecting and recycling appliances for utilities and other
sponsors of energy efficiency programs.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Selling
byproduct materials from appliances that we recycle, including appliances
collected through our ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">We
were incorporated in Minnesota in 1983, although through our predecessors we
began our appliance retail and recycling business in 1976.&#160; Our principal office is located at 7400
Excelsior Boulevard, Minneapolis, Minnesota, 55426-4517.&#160; References herein to our Company include our
operating subsidiaries.&#160; (See &#147;Exhibits.&#148;)</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Industry
Background</font></b><a name="IndustryBackground_005631"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the United States,
more than 700 million major household appliances are currently in use.&#160; These appliances include:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="76%" style="border-collapse:collapse;margin-left:.5in;width:76.66%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="28%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:28.8%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Refrigerators</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="71%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:71.2%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Washers</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="28%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:28.8%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Freezers</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="71%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:71.2%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Dryers</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="28%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:28.8%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Ranges/ovens</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="71%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:71.2%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Room&nbsp;air
  conditioners</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="28%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:28.8%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Dishwashers</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="71%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:71.2%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Dehumidifiers</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="28%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:28.8%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Microwave ovens</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="71%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:71.2%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Humidifiers</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2008, factory
shipments of major appliances totaled more than 68 million units in the
U.S.&#160; Total sales of the top 100 major
appliance retailers in 2007 were over $25 billion, a slight decline compared to
2006.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">With every new appliance
sale comes the potential for disposal of the unit that was replaced.&#160; Improper disposal of old appliances threatens
air, ground and water resources because many types of major appliances contain
substances that can damage the environment.&#160;
These harmful materials include:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.3pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Polychlorinated
biphenyls (&#147;PCBs&#148;), which have toxic effects on humans and animals.&#160; Although the Environmental Protection Agency
(&#147;EPA&#148;) banned the production of PCBs in 1979, it allowed manufacturers to use
their remaining inventories of PCB-containing components.&#160; Consequently, some old room air conditioners
and microwave ovens have capacitors that contain PCBs, which can contaminate
groundwater when released.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.3pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.3pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Mercury,
which easily enters the body through absorption, inhalation or ingestion,
potentially causing neurological damage.&#160;
Freezers and washers may have mercury-containing switches in their lids.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.3pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.3pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Chlorofluorocarbon,
hydrochlorofluorocarbon, and hydrofluorocarbon (collectively, &#147;CFC&#148;)
refrigerants, which cause long-term damage to the earth&#146;s ozone layer and may
contribute to global climate change.&#160;
Refrigerators, freezers, room air conditioners and dehumidifiers
commonly contain CFC refrigerants.&#160; CFCs
are also used as blowing agents in the polyurethane foam insulation of
refrigeration appliances.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.3pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-17.6pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Other
materials, such as oil and sulfur dioxide, that are harmful when released into
the environment.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='3',FILE='C:\JMS\105538\09-1423-1\task3437901\1423-1-ba-01.htm',USER='105538',CD='Mar 21 00:45 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The federal government
requires the recovery of CFC refrigerants upon appliance disposal and also
regulates the management of hazardous materials found in appliances.&#160; Most state and local governments have also
enacted laws affecting how their residents dispose of unwanted appliances.&#160; For example, some areas restrict landfills
and scrap metal processors from accepting appliances unless the units have been
processed to remove environmentally harmful materials.&#160; As a result, old appliances usually cannot be
discarded directly through ordinary solid waste systems.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition to these
solid waste management and environmental issues, energy conservation is another
compelling reason for proper disposal of old appliances.&#160; Refrigerators manufactured in the 1960s and
early 1970s use up to 1,750 kilowatt-hours of electricity each year.&#160; By 2001, federal standards enacted over the
years lowered the average annual energy use per new unit to less than 500
kilowatt-hours.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To encourage the
permanent removal of energy inefficient appliances from use, many electric
utility companies sponsor programs through which their residential customers
can retire working refrigerators, freezers and room air conditioners.&#160; Utility companies often provide assistance
and incentives for consumers to discontinue use of a surplus appliance or to
replace their old, inefficient appliances with newer, more efficient models.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The EPA has been supportive of efforts by electric utilities and other
entities that sponsor appliance recycling programs to ensure that the collected
units are managed in an environmentally sound manner.&#160; In October&nbsp;2006, the EPA launched the
Responsible Appliance Disposal (&#147;RAD&#148;) Program, a voluntary partnership program
designed to help protect the ozone layer and reduce emissions of greenhouse
gases.&#160; Through the RAD program, partners
use best practices to recover ozone-depleting chemicals and other harmful
materials from old refrigerators, freezers, room air conditioners and
dehumidifiers. We were active participants in helping to design the RAD program
and currently collect and submit annual reports to the EPA to document the
environmental benefits our utility customers have achieved through their
recycling programs.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Company
Background</font></b><a name="CompanyBackground_005634"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We started our business
in 1976 as a used-appliance retailer that reconditioned old appliances to sell
at our stores.&#160; Under contract with
national and regional retailers of new appliances, such as Sears and Montgomery
Ward, we collected the replaced appliance from the customer&#146;s residence when
one of their stores delivered a new appliance in the Minneapolis/St. Paul,
Miami or Atlanta market.&#160; The old
appliances that we could not sell in our stores were sold to scrap metal
processors.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the late 1980s,
stricter environmental regulations began to affect the disposal of unwanted
appliances, and we were no longer able to take appliances that contained
hazardous components to a scrap metal processor.&#160; At that time, we began to develop systems and
equipment to remove the harmful materials so that metal processors would accept
the appliance shells for processing.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We then offered our
services for disposing of appliances in an environmentally sound manner to
appliance manufacturers and retailers, waste hauling companies, rental property
managers, local governments and the public.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Appliance
Recycling for Energy Efficiency Programs</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 1989, we began
contracting with electric utility companies to provide turnkey appliance
recycling services to support their energy conservation efforts.&#160; Since that time, we have provided our
services to more than eighty utilities throughout the U.S. and Canada.&#160; Current programs include:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>A
contract with Southern California Edison (&#147;Edison&#148;) to handle refrigerator and
freezer recycling operations, extending Edison&#146;s 2006-2008 appliance recycling
program, which covers fifty percent of the previous southern and central
California territories that we handled under Edison&#146;s 2004-2005 program.&#160; The contract runs through December&nbsp;2009.&#160; Under terms of this agreement, Edison is
responsible for advertising the program.&#160;
We expect the contract will be renewed for 2010 and 2011.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>A
bridge contract with San Diego Gas&nbsp;&amp; Electric (&#147;SDG&amp;E&#148;) for their
2006-2008 program to provide refrigerator, freezer and room air conditioner
recycling services for their residential and small commercial customers.&#160; The contract runs through December&nbsp;2009.&#160; Under terms of this agreement, we are
responsible for advertising the program.&#160;
We expect the contract will be renewed for 2010 and 2011.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>An
expansion of our contract with Southern California Public Power Authority (&#147;SCPPA&#148;),
which sponsors a program to replace and recycle old, inefficient refrigerators
owned by low income residents in the city.&#160;
We currently perform these services for all participating members of
SCPPA, including the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (&#147;LADWP&#148;).&#160; The contract runs through June&nbsp;2010.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>An
agreement with WPPI Energy (&#147;WPPI&#148;) to provide appliance recycling services
through 2009.&#160; WPPI is a regional power
company owned by 50 municipalities that operate electric utilities.&#160; They supply power to 190,000 homes and
businesses in Wisconsin, Upper Michigan and Iowa.&#160; WPPI is responsible for advertising this
program.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>An
agreement entered into in December&nbsp;2004 with Austin Energy in Texas to
manage appliance recycling operations in their service territory during
2005.&#160; The contract was initially
extended through 2006, 2007 and 2008, and was extended again until December&nbsp;2009
with two one-year extensions.&#160; In 2008,
Austin Energy initiated a refrigerator replacement program for which we
purchase and install new appliances and recycle the old units.&#160; Austin Energy is the nation&#146;s ninth largest
community-owned electric utility, serving 388,000 customers within the City of
Austin and surrounding counties.&#160; Austin
Energy is responsible for advertising this program.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">6.</font><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font face="Times New Roman">A contract
announced in June&nbsp;2007 with Ontario Power Authority (&#147;OPA&#148;) in Ontario,
Canada, to recycle refrigerators, freezers and room air conditioners throughout
the province.&#160; The program is
administered by OPA and managed by more than seventy local electric
distribution companies.&#160; This contract
runs through the end of 2010.&#160; OPA is
responsible for advertising this program.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">7.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>A
subcontracting agreement with Conservation Services Group (&#147;CSG&#148;) to provide
appliance recycling services to Ameren Illinois Utilities (AIU) residential
customers in Illinois.&#160; The program is
administered by CSG and the contract is through May&nbsp;2011. We are
responsible for advertising the program at the direction of CSG.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">8.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>In
November&nbsp;2008, we entered into contracts with American Electric Power (&#147;AEP&#148;)
Texas North Company (&#147;TNC&#148;), Texas Central Company (&#147;TCC&#148;) and Southwestern
Electric Power Company (&#147;SWEPCO&#148;) to manage appliance recycling for
approximately 930,000 residential and 184,000 commercial customers through December&nbsp;2009.&#160; We are responsible for advertising the
programs.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">9.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Additionally,
we have appliance recycling contracts with Minnesota Power and City Water Light
and Power (&#147;CWLP&#148;) of Springfield, Illinois.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the past two years, we
have seen a heightened interest from electric utilities that recognize the
effectiveness of programs to recycle energy inefficient appliances.&#160; We are aggressively pursuing this segment of
customers in 2009 and expect that we will continue to submit proposals for
various new utility appliance recycling programs throughout the year.&#160; However, we still have a limited ability to
project revenues from new utility programs in 2009.&#160; We cannot predict recycling volumes or if we
will be successful obtaining new contracts.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets</font></i></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">When electric utilities
began to scale back or discontinue their programs with us in the mid-1990s due
to the potential of wide-scale industry deregulation, we increased our
marketing to appliance manufacturers and retailers, waste haulers and property
management companies.&#160; We also concentrated
on strengthening our retail appliance operations, and in 1998, we introduced
ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets to focus on selling new appliances.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of March&nbsp;2009, ApplianceSmart was operating twenty factory
outlets: six in the Minneapolis/St. Paul market; one in Rochester, Minn.; four
in the Columbus, Ohio, market; six in the Atlanta market; and three in the San
Antonio, Texas, market.&#160; We believe we
are one of the nation&#146;s leading retailers of special-buy household appliances,
primarily those manufactured by Whirlpool, General Electric and
Frigidaire.&#160; These special-buy
appliances&#151;which include close-outs, factory overruns, floor samples, returned
or exchanged items, and scratch-and-dent units&#151;typically are not integrated
into </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='5',FILE='C:\JMS\105538\09-1423-1\task3437901\1423-1-ba-01.htm',USER='105538',CD='Mar 21 00:45 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table
of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">manufacturers&#146; normal distribution channels.&#160; In the appliance industry, these appliances
require a different method of management.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The appliances we sell at
ApplianceSmart are new, not used.&#160; Some
are in the carton while others are out of the carton.&#160; One example of this type of special-buy
product involves manufacturer redesign, in which a current model is updated to
include a few new features and is then assigned a new model number.&#160; Because the manufacturer ships only the
latest models to retailers, a large quantity of the older model remains in the
manufacturer&#146;s inventory.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For many years,
manufacturers relied on small appliance dealers to buy this product to sell in
their stores.&#160; However, today these small
retailers are struggling to compete with large appliance chains: the top 10
retailers are estimated to control 80 percent of the appliance sales market.&#160; At the same time, the expansion of big-box
retailers that sell appliances has created a dramatic increase in the number of
special-buy units, further straining the traditional outlet system for these
appliances.&#160; Because these special-buy
appliances have value, manufacturers and retailers need an efficient management
system to recover their worth.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Manufacturer
Supply</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have entered into
reverse logistics contracts with the following five major manufacturers:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Whirlpool</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>General
Electric (&#147;GE&#148;)</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Frigidaire</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Danby</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Bosch</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">These agreements have
been extended through 2009 and in addition to the reverse logistics contracts,
we are also an authorized new-appliance dealer for these five manufacturers.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">There are no guarantees
on the number of units that any of the manufacturers will sell us; however, we
believe that purchases from these five manufacturers will provide an adequate
supply of high-quality appliances for our ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Key components of our
current agreements include:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>We
have no guarantees for the number or type of appliances that we have to
purchase.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>The
agreement may be terminated by either party with 30 days&#146; prior written notice.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>We
have agreed to indemnify certain manufacturers for certain claims, allegations
or losses concerning the appliances we sell.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; In October
2008, we signed an agreement to carry appliances manufactured by Bosch.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.55in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Subsidiaries</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ARCA Canada Inc., a
Canadian corporation, is a wholly owned subsidiary.&#160; ARCA Canada was formed in September&nbsp;2006
to provide turnkey appliance recycling services for electric utility energy
efficiency programs.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We were a sixty percent
owner in North America Appliance Company, LLC (&#147;NAACO&#148;).&#160; NAACO was formed and commenced operations in June&nbsp;2003
and was a retailer of special-buy appliances in Texas.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We were a sixty percent
owner in Productos Duraderos de Norte America (&#147;PDN&#148;), a Mexican
corporation.&#160; PDN was acquired in September&nbsp;2006
and refurbished room air conditioners for sale through our NAACO operation in
McAllen, Texas, and through our ApplianceSmart Factory Outlet stores.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the fourth quarter
of 2008, we planned and executed the shutdown of our NAACO and PDN
subsidiaries.&#160; NAACO and PDN were not
operating as planned and were no longer economically viable.&#160; In 2008, our supply of room air </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">6</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">conditioners from a major
manufacturer was depleted, no longer providing refurbishment opportunities for
PDN and revenues for NAACO, which was the basis for our investment in these
businesses.&#160; We will not have any
continuing involvement or significant continuing cash flows in these
businesses.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Growth
Strategy</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Larger factory outlet
facilities offer consumers a wider selection of appliances than smaller stores
do and are more efficient for us to operate.&#160;
For these reasons, we intend to continue to focus our retail sales
operations on larger facilities.&#160; We
would consider opening new stores primarily in markets in which we currently
have operations to benefit from operational and marketing efficiencies of
scale.&#160; However, we will study other
major consumer markets throughout the United States with the possibility of
expanding our retail stores to new markets.&#160;
We evaluate demographic, economic and financial information as well as
the facility and proposed lease terms when considering a new store location.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have also seen renewed
interest from sponsors of energy efficiency programs across the country that
recognize the effectiveness of recycling energy inefficient appliances.&#160; We are aggressively pursuing this segment of
customers in 2009 and expect that we will continue to submit proposals for
various new utility appliance recycling programs.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2008, we entered into
an agreement to become the exclusive North American distributor for UNTHA
Recycling Technology (URT), one of the world&#146;s leading manufacturers of
technologically advanced refrigerator recycling systems and recycling
facilities for electrical household appliances and electronic scrap.&#160; In addition to marketing these systems to the
recycling industry, we intend to install a URT system at one of our higher
volume appliance recycling facilities.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Customers
and Source of Supply</font></b><a name="CustomersAndSourceOfSupply_005640"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We offer reverse
logistics services to manufacturers and retailers that need an efficient way to
manage appliances that fall outside their normal distribution and sales
channels.&#160; We also provide services for
electric utility companies that offer their customers appliance recycling
programs as an energy conservation measure.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Appliance Manufacturers</font></u>:</i>&#160; We work with appliance manufacturers,
including Whirlpool, Frigidaire, GE and Danby, to acquire the product we sell
at our ApplianceSmart Factory Outlet stores.&#160;
We purchase special-buy appliances, such as discontinued models and
factory overruns, and sell the product at a significant discount to full retail
prices.&#160; In addition, our participation
in a national buying cooperative enables us to purchase new in-the-carton
appliances to fill out our mix of product.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Although we believe that
our current sources for appliances are adequate to supply our retail stores and
allow us to grow our sales, we face the risk that one or more of these sources
could be lost.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Electric Utility Companies</font></u>:</i>&#160; We contract with utility companies and other
sponsors of energy efficiency programs to provide a full range of appliance
recycling services to help them achieve their energy savings goals.&#160; The contracts usually have terms of one to
four years, with provisions for renewal at the option of the utility.&#160; Under some contracts, we manage all aspects
of the appliance recycling program.&#160;
Under other contracts, we provide only specified services, such as
collection and recycling.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our contracts with
electric utility customers prohibit us from repairing and selling appliances we
receive through their programs.&#160; Because
the intent of the program is to conserve electricity, we have instituted
tracking and auditing procedures to assure our customers that those appliances
do not return to use.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our pricing for energy
efficiency program contracts is on a per appliance basis and depends upon
several factors, including:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Total number of
appliances expected to be processed.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Length of the
contract term.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Specific services
the utility selects us to provide.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Market factors,
including labor rates and transportation costs.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">7</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Currently, we have
contracts for 2009 with the following utilities to handle recycling operations
in their service territories:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Southern
California Edison</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>San
Diego Gas&nbsp;&amp; Electric</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>WPPI
Energy</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Austin
Energy</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Southern
California Public Power Authority</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">6.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Ontario
Power Authority</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">7.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Minnesota
Power</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">8.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Oncor</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">9.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>AEP
Texas</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">10.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Ameren
Illinois Utilities</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">11.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>City
Water Light&nbsp;&amp; Power</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">12.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Several
other municipal utilities</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-9.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Company
Operations</font></b><a name="CompanyOperations_005644"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We provide a full range
of appliance recycling support services for energy efficiency programs in the
U.S. and Canada.&#160; We also purchase major
appliances, primarily from appliance manufacturers, to sell through our
ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Many of the appliances we
receive from manufacturers are still in the factory carton and ready to
sell.&#160; Other appliances need repair or
cosmetic work before we deliver them to our ApplianceSmart retail outlets.&#160; All appliances we sell are under warranty and
carry a 100 percent money-back guarantee.&#160;
We also offer extended warranties, appliance delivery, factory-trained
technician service and recycling of customers&#146; old appliances.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Appliances that do not
meet our quality standards for sale at our ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets and
appliances collected through utility customers&#146; energy conservation programs
must be recycled to prevent re-use.&#160; We
process and recycle these units using environmentally sound systems and
techniques.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In our recycling
operation, our Company-trained technicians first inspect and categorize each
appliance to identify the types of hazardous materials it contains.&#160; We then process the appliances to remove and
manage the environmentally hazardous substances according to all federal, state
and local regulations.&#160; We deliver the
processed appliances to local scrap processing facilities, where they shred and
recycle the metals.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Although the potential of
industry deregulation negatively affected our business with electric utilities
in the middle to late 1990s, we are seeing renewed interest in appliance
recycling programs for energy efficiency.&#160;
We enter 2009 serving seven major utility programs and several smaller
ones.&#160; We are aggressively pursuing
additional utility customers, but have a limited ability to project revenues
from new utility programs in 2009.&#160; We
cannot predict recycling volumes or if we will be successful obtaining new
contracts.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have focused on a
carefully managed growth plan to strengthen our retail operations.&#160; We opened four new factory outlets in 2008
and one in 2009 and will continue to open factory outlet stores in convenient,
high-traffic locations.&#160; To supply our
stores with a wide selection of appliances, we will continue to seek additional
sources of product.&#160; We currently operate
twenty factory outlets in strategic markets throughout the U.S., and we believe
that the growth of our retail business in the near future will likely occur
through opening new ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets in our existing markets.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Principal
Products and Services</font></b><a name="PrincipalProductsAndServices_005646"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We generate revenues from
three sources: retail, recycling and byproduct.&#160;
Retail revenues are generated through the sale of new appliances at our
ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets.&#160;
Recycling revenues are generated by charging fees for collecting and
recycling appliances for utilities and other sponsors of energy efficiency
programs.&#160; Byproduct revenues are
generated by </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">8</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">selling scrap materials,
such as metal and plastics, and reclaimed CFC refrigerants from appliances we
collect and recycle, including those from our ApplianceSmart stores.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The table below
reflects the percentage of total revenues from continuing operations from each
source for the past two fiscal years.&#160;
See also &#147;Management&#146;s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition
and Results of Operations.&#148;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">During fiscal years 2008 and 2007, we operated two reportable segments:
retail and recycling.&#160; The retail segment
is comprised of sales generated through our ApplianceSmart Factory
Outlets.&#160; Our recycling segment includes
all fees charged for collecting, recycling and installing appliances for utilities
and other customers and includes byproduct revenue, which is generated
primarily through the recycling of appliances.&#160;
Financial information about our segments is included in &#147;Item 7.
Management&#146;s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of
Operations,&#148; and Note 12 of &#147;Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements.&#148;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Sales and
Marketing</font></b><a name="SalesAndMarketing_005648"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We use a variety of
methods to promote awareness of our products and services.&#160; We believe that we are recognized as a leader
in the recycling industry and in special-buy appliance retailing.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our ApplianceSmart
Factory Outlet store concept includes establishing large showrooms in
metropolitan locations where we can offer consumers a selection of hundreds of
appliances at each of our stores.&#160; Our
visual branding consists of ample display of product, manufacturers&#146; signage
and custom-designed ApplianceSmart materials in red, white and blue.&#160; We advertise our stores through print media,
television, radio and direct mail.&#160;
Through www.ApplianceSmart.com, consumers can also learn more about us
on the Internet.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We market our services to
electric utility companies and other sponsors of energy efficiency programs by
contacting prospective customers directly, delivering educational presentations
at conferences for energy efficiency professionals, participating in utility
industry trade shows, networking with key affiliates of electric power and
environmental associations, and promoting our corporate website at
www.arcainc.com.&#160; We submit sales
proposals for our services to interested parties and in response to
utility-issued requests to bid.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Seasonality</font></b><a name="Seasonality_005649"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We experience some
seasonality in retail revenues, with revenues in the second and third calendar
quarters being slightly higher than revenues in the first and fourth calendar
quarters.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Traditionally, our
electric utility customers with multi-year programs have rolled out their
programs towards the end of the first calendar quarter.&#160; Promotional activities are strong during the
second and third calendar quarters, leading to higher customer demand for
services during that time period.&#160; As a
result, we experience a surge in business during the second and third calendar
quarters, which declines through the fourth and first calendar quarters until
advertising activities resume.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:-.15pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">Competition<a name="Competition_005650"></a></font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:-.15pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our retail competition
comes mainly from new-appliance and other special-buy retailers.&#160; Each ApplianceSmart Factory Outlet competes
with local and national retail appliance chains, as well as with independently
owned retailers.&#160; Many of these retailers
have been in business longer than we have and may have significantly greater
assets.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">9</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='9',FILE='C:\JMS\105538\09-1423-1\task3437901\1423-1-ba-01.htm',USER='105538',CD='Mar 21 00:45 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Many factors, including
obtaining adequate resources to create and support the infrastructure required
to operate large-scale appliance recycling programs, affect competition in the
appliance recycling industry.&#160; We generally
compete for contracts with several other national appliance recycling
businesses and energy services management companies.&#160; We also compete with small hauling or
recycling companies based in the program&#146;s service territory; however, these
companies generally are not able to offer the full range of services that we
provide.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We expect our primary
competition for appliance recycling contracts with existing and new customers
to come from a variety of sources, including:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Existing recycling
companies.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Entrepreneurs
entering the appliance recycling business.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Energy management
consultants.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Major waste hauling
companies.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Scrap metal
processors.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition, utility
companies and other customers may choose to provide all or some of the services
required to operate their appliance recycling programs internally rather than
contracting with outside vendors.&#160; We
have no assurance that we will be able to compete profitably in any of our
chosen markets.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Government
Regulation</font></b><a name="GovernmentRegulation_005653"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Federal, state and local
governments regulate appliance collection, recycling and sales activities.&#160; While some requirements apply nationwide,
others vary by market.&#160; The many laws and
regulations that affect appliance recycling include landfill disposal
restrictions, hazardous waste management requirements and air quality
standards.&#160; For example, the 1990
Amendments to the Clean Air Act prohibit the venting of CFC and CFC-substitute
refrigerants while servicing or disposing of appliances.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Each of our recycling
facilities maintains the appropriate registrations, permits and licenses for
operating at its location.&#160; We register
our recycling centers as hazardous waste generators with the EPA and obtain all
appropriate regional and local licenses for managing hazardous wastes.&#160; Licensed hazardous waste companies transport
and recycle or dispose of the hazardous materials we generate.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have been recognized
for our work in protecting the environment from the harmful effects of improper
appliance disposal.&#160; In 2004, the EPA
awarded us, along with our customer Southern California Edison, the
Stratospheric Ozone Protection Award for the environmentally responsible manner
in which we collect and dispose of appliances.&#160;
In 2007, we were again recognized by the EPA with a Best of the Best Stratospheric
Ozone Protection Award as part of an appliance recycling team responsible for &#147;the
most exceptional global contributions in the first two decades of the Montreal
Protocol.&#148;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2007, we became a
founding reporter of The Climate Registry, an organization that provides
information regarding the measurement and reporting of greenhouse gas emissions
to various governmental and private agencies and businesses.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our retail stores obtain
all business licenses, sales tax licenses and permits required for their
locations.&#160; Our delivery and collection
vehicles comply with all Department of Transportation licensing requirements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Although we believe that
further governmental regulation of the appliance recycling industry could have
a positive effect on us, we cannot foretell the direction of future
legislation.&#160; Under some circumstances,
for example, further regulation could materially increase our operational
costs.&#160; In addition, under some
circumstances we may be subject to contingent liabilities because we handle
hazardous materials.&#160; We believe we are
in compliance with all government regulations regarding the handling of
hazardous materials, and we have environmental insurance to mitigate the impact
of any potential contingent liability.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">10</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
