  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.88%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">76.2</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.12%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.88%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">71.9</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.9</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="60%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:60.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Recycling</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">29.6</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">25.2</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">17.6</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="60%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:60.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Byproduct</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.2</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.7</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">94.0</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="60%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:60.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.12%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" style="border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.88%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-align:right;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">111.0</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.12%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" style="border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.88%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">99.8</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">11.2</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">%</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our total revenues for
2008 increased $11.2 million or 11.2% from $99.8 million in 2007.&#160; Retail sales accounted for 69% of total sales
for 2008 compared to 72% in 2007.&#160; We
experienced growth in all of our revenue streams but do not expect this level
of growth in 2009 due to the economic slowdown and decline in scrap metal prices
from the first three quarters of 2008.&#160;
We expect that scrap metal prices will remain depressed throughout 2009.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Retail Revenues</font></i>.&#160; Our retail revenues for 2008 increased $4.3
million or 5.9% from $71.9 million in 2007.&#160;
The increase in retail revenues was primarily due to opening four new factory
outlet stores in 2008.&#160; These new stores
contributed $6.5 million of additional revenues in 2008 compared to 2007.&#160; The revenue growth from our new stores was
partially offset by the closure of our California factory outlet in September&nbsp;2007.&#160; Our comparable store sales of the fourteen
ApplianceSmart Factory Outlets that were open during the entire years of 2008
and 2007 were down 2.5% or $1.7 million.&#160;
The primary decrease in comparable store sales occurred in the fourth
quarter of 2008.&#160; Prior to the fourth
quarter, our comparable sales were up 2.6% for the first three quarters of 2008
compared to the same period in 2007.&#160; In
the fourth quarter of 2008 our comparable store sales were down 16.7% primarily
due to two factors: 1) the fourth quarter of 2007 was positively impacted by a
large close-out purchase from a major manufacturer that resulted in strong
customer sales.&#160; This purchasing
opportunity did not exist in 2008, and 2) the overall retail economy is experiencing
a nationwide slowdown and cutback in consumer spending.&#160; We expect the economic slowdown and cutback
in consumer spending to continue through 2009, negatively impacting our
sales.&#160; We also plan to slow down the
opening of new stores in 2009 or until we see a pickup in consumer spending.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our factory outlets carry a wide range of new
in-the-box and special-buy appliances, which include manufacturer closeouts,
factory overruns, floor samples, returned or exchanged items, open-carton
items, and scratch and dent appliances.&#160;
All these appliances are new.&#160;
Some are in the carton while others are out of the carton.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We continue to purchase
the majority of both new in-the-box and special-buy appliances from Whirlpool,
GE and Frigidaire.&#160; We have no minimum
purchase requirements with any of these manufacturers.&#160; We believe purchases from these three
manufacturers will provide an adequate supply of high-quality appliances for
our retail factory outlets; however, there is a risk that one or more of these
sources could be curtailed or lost.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">22</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='22',FILE='C:\JMS\sjayara\09-1423-1\task3432211\1423-1-ba-05.htm',USER='105898',CD='Mar 19 01:09 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table
of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Recycling Revenues</font></i>.&#160; Our recycling revenues for 2008 increased
$4.4 million or 17.6% from $25.2 million in 2007.&#160; The increase was primarily due to recycling
more appliances under our Ontario Power Authority and Southern California
Public Power Authority contracts in 2008 compared to 2007.&#160; We are aggressively pursuing new appliance
recycling programs but cannot predict if we will be successful in signing new
contracts or renewing existing contracts.</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Byproduct Revenues</font></i>.&#160; Our byproduct revenues for 2008 increased
$2.5 million or 94.0% from $2.7 million in 2007.&#160; The increase was due primarily to higher
scrap metal prices we received during the first three quarters of 2008 and, to
a lesser extent, increased recycling volumes.&#160;
During the fourth quarter of 2008, there was a significant decline in
the demand and prices we received for scrap materials.&#160; We expect that scrap material prices will
stay depressed throughout 2009.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Gross
Profit</font></i>.</b>&#160; Our
overall gross profit increased $3.1 million or 9.5% from $32.5 million in
2007.&#160; Our gross profit as a percentage
of total revenue was 32% in 2008 compared to 33% in 2007.&#160; Recycling gross profit percentages are
typically higher than retail gross profit percentages. Our gross profit as a
percentage of total revenues for future periods can be affected favorably or
unfavorably by numerous factors, including:</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>The mix of retail
products we sell.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>The prices at which
we purchase product from the major manufacturers who supply product to us.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>The volume of
appliances we receive through our recycling contracts.</p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>The volume and
price of scrap metals, plastics and reclaimed CFCs.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Unless we can
significantly increase our appliance purchasing volume, resulting in a higher
level rebates, or sign substantial recycling contracts utilizing our current
recycling facilities, gross profit percentages will remain flat in 2009.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:0pt;">Selling, General and
Administrative Expenses</font></i></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:0pt;">.</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&#160;
Our selling, general and administrative (&#147;SG&amp;A&#148;) expenses for 2008
increased $3.2 million or 11.3% from $28.4 million in 2007.&#160; Our SG&amp;A expenses as a percentage of
total revenues remained unchanged at 28% in 2008 compared to 2007.&#160; Selling expenses increased $1.4 million to
$19.9 million in 2008 from $18.5 million in 2007.&#160; The increase in selling expenses was due
primarily to opening four new factory outlet stores in 2008 and the full-year
impact of opening our factory outlet store in Ohio in December&nbsp;2007.&#160; General and administrative expenses increased
$1.9 million to $11.7 million in 2008 from $9.8 million in 2007.&#160; The increase was due primarily to additional
employee compensation, share-based compensation and legal fees associated with
the legal proceedings discussed in Part&nbsp;I, Item 3, &#147;Legal Proceedings,&#148;
and Note 8 of &#147;Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements.&#148;&#160; We expect SG&amp;A expenses to decrease
slightly as a percentage of total revenues in 2009 due to cost saving initiatives.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Interest
Expense</font></i></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Interest expense decreased $0.1 million to
$1.4 million in 2008 compared to $1.5 million in 2007.&#160; The decrease was primarily due to the decline
in the weighted average interest rate on our line of credit from 10.58% in 2007
to 6.83% in 2008.&#160; We cannot predict what
will happen with interest rates in 2009.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Provision
for Income Taxes</font></i>.</b>&#160;
Our provision for income taxes for 2008 increased $0.5 million to $0.7
million in 2008 compared to $0.2 million in 2007.&#160; The increase is primarily due to taxable
income from our Canadian subsidiary.&#160; As
of January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007, we recorded cumulative
valuation allowances of $2.2 million and $2.3 million, respectively, against
our U.S. net deferred tax assets due to the uncertainty of their
realization.&#160; The realization of deferred
tax assets is dependent upon sufficient future taxable income during the
periods when deductible temporary differences and carryforwards are expected to
be available to reduce taxable income.&#160;
We do not expect a significant increase or decrease in our provision for
income taxes in 2009.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Discontinued
Operations</font></i>.</b>&#160; During
the fourth quarter of 2008, we planned and executed the shutdown of our NAACO
and PDN operations.&#160; NAACO and PDN were
not operating as planned and were no longer economically viable.&#160; In 2008, our supply of room air conditioners
from a major manufacturer was depleted, no longer providing refurbishment
opportunities for PDN and revenues for NAACO, which was the basis for our
investment in these businesses.&#160; We will
not have any continuing involvement or significant continuing cash flows in
these businesses.&#160; The results of
operations for NAACO and PDN were included in our retail segment and have been
reclassified as discontinued operations.&#160;
During the fiscal years ended January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007, NAACO and PDN had revenues of $0.8 million and $1.0 million,
respectively, and income (loss) before taxes of ($0.9) million and $0.1
million, respectively.&#160; We recorded a
loss on the </p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">23</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='23',FILE='C:\JMS\sjayara\09-1423-1\task3432211\1423-1-ba-05.htm',USER='105898',CD='Mar 19 01:09 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">disposal of NAACO
and PDN of $0.7 million, net of taxes in 2008, which consisted of termination
costs and the write-down of certain assets to fair value upon the abandonment
of the operations in December.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Principal Sources and Uses of Liquidity.</font></i></b><b><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our principal sources of liquidity are cash from
operations and borrowings under our line of credit.&#160; Our principal liquidity requirements consist
of long-term debt obligations, capital expenditures and working capital.&#160; Our cash and cash equivalents as of January&nbsp;3,
2009 were $3.5 million compared to $2.8 million as of December&nbsp;29,
2007.&#160; Our working capital increased to
$5.8 million for the year ended January&nbsp;3, 2009 compared to $5.1 million
for the year ended December&nbsp;29, 2007.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Net Cash Provided by (Used in)
Operating Activities</font></i></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Our net cash provided by operating activities
was $1.6 million in 2008 compared to net cash used in operating activities of
$6.2 million in 2007.&#160; During 2008,
inventories increased by $5.0 million, primarily due to opening four new
factory outlet stores, compared to a $3.1 million increase in 2007.&#160; During 2008, accounts receivable decreased
$3.6 million, primarily due to the timing of collections of recycling
receivables which was largely the cause of the increase of $7.7 million in
2007.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Net
Cash Used in Investing Activities</font></i>.</b>&#160; Our net cash used in investing activities was
$0.8 million in 2008 compared to $0.9 million in 2007.&#160; The net cash used in investing activities in
2008 and 2007 was primarily related to the purchase of computer equipment,
phone systems, leasehold improvements to support our new store openings and
recycling equipment to support recycling contracts.&#160; We did not have any material purchase
commitments for assets as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 or through March&nbsp;2,
2009.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Net
Cash Provided by Financing Activities</font></i>.</b>&#160; Our net cash provided by financing activities
was $0.4 million in 2008 compared to $7.1 million in 2007.&#160; The net cash provided by financing activities
in 2008 was primarily due to borrowings under our line of credit, partially
offset by payments on our long-term debt obligations.&#160; The net cash provided by financing activities
in 2007 was primarily due to increased borrowings under the line of credit for
inventory purchases and stock option exercises, offset by payments on long-term
debt obligations.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Outstanding
Indebtedness</font></i>.</b>&#160; As
of January&nbsp;3, 2009, we had an $18.0 million line of credit with a
lender.&#160; The line was increased from
$16.0 million to $18.0 million on February&nbsp;5, 2008.&#160; The interest rate on the line as of January&nbsp;3,
2009 was 6.25% (the greater of prime plus 1.50 percentage points or 6.25%).&#160; The amount of borrowings available under the
line of credit is based on a formula using receivables and inventories.&#160; We may not have access to the full $18.0
million line of credit due to the formula using our receivables and
inventories.&#160; The outstanding balance and
unused borrowing capacity were $14.5 million and $0.4 million, respectively, as
of January&nbsp;3, 2009.&#160; At March&nbsp;2,
2009, borrowings of $12.2 million were
outstanding under the line of credit, and we had unused borrowing capacity of $0.2 million.&#160;
The line of credit has a stated maturity date of December&nbsp;31,
2010, if not renewed, and provides that the lender may demand payment in full
of the entire outstanding balance of the loan at any time.&#160; The line of credit is secured by
substantially all our assets and requires minimum monthly interest payments of
$58,000, regardless of the outstanding principal balance.&#160; The lender is also secured by an inventory
repurchase agreement with Whirlpool Corporation for purchases from Whirlpool
only.&#160; The line requires that we meet
certain financial covenants, provides payment penalties for noncompliance and
prepayment, limits the amount of other debt we can incur, limits the amount of
spending on fixed assets and prohibits payments of dividends.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3, 2009 we were not in
compliance with certain financial covenants and received a waiver from the
lender.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">In September&nbsp;2008, we entered
into a master equipment lease with a lender providing up to $0.3 million in
available funds.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3,
2009, we had utilized the entire lease line to fund equipment for the new
retail outlets opened in December&nbsp;2008 and January&nbsp;2009.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">As of January&nbsp;3, 2009, we had
long-term obligations of $5.5 million consisting of mortgages on our Minnesota
and California buildings along with various financings, primarily consisting of
capital leases.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">24</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">We believe, based on the
anticipated sales per retail store, the anticipated revenues from our recycling
contracts and our anticipated gross profit, that our cash balance, anticipated
funds generated from operations and our current line of credit will be
sufficient to finance our operations, long-term debt obligations and capital
expenditures through December&nbsp;2009.&#160;
Our total capital requirements for 2009 will depend upon, among other
things as discussed below, the number and size of retail stores operating
during the fiscal year and the recycling volumes generated from recycling
contracts in 2009.&#160; Currently, we have
twenty retail stores and six recycling centers in operation.&#160; We may need additional capital to finance our
operations if our revenues are lower than anticipated or our expenses are
higher than anticipated, or we pursue new opportunities.&#160; Sources of additional financing, if needed in
the future, may include further debt financing or the sale of equity (common or
preferred stock) or other financing opportunities.&#160; There can be no assurance that such
additional sources of financing will be available on terms satisfactory to us
or permitted by our current debt agreement.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="letter-spacing:-.1pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">APPLICATION OF CRITICAL
ACCOUNTING POLICIES</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our discussion of the
financial condition and results of operations is based upon our consolidated
financial statements, which have been prepared in conformity with accounting
principles generally accepted in the United States.&#160; The preparation of our consolidated financial
statements requires management to make estimates and assumptions that affect
the reported amounts of assets and liabilities, revenues and expenses, and
related disclosure of any contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the
financial statements.&#160; Management
regularly reviews its estimates and assumptions, which are based on historical
factors and other factors that are believed to be relevant under the
circumstances.&#160; Actual results may differ
from these estimates under different assumptions, estimates or conditions.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Critical accounting
policies are defined as those that are reflective of significant judgments and
uncertainties, and potentially result in materially different results under
different assumptions and conditions.&#160;
See Note 3 of &#147;Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements&#148; for
additional disclosure of the application of these and other accounting
policies.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Revenue
Recognition.</font></i></b>&#160; We
recognize revenue from appliance sales in the period the consumer purchases and
pays for the appliance, net of an allowance for estimated returns.&#160; We recognize revenue from appliance recycling
when we collect and process a unit.&#160; We
recognize byproduct revenue upon shipment.&#160;
We recognize revenue on extended warranties with retained service
obligations on a straight-line basis over the period of the warranty in
accordance with FASB Technical Bulletin 90-1, <i>Accounting
for Separately Priced Extended Warranty and Product Maintenance Contracts</i>.&#160; On extended warranty arrangements that we
sell but others service for a fixed portion of the warranty sales price, we
recognize revenue for the net amount retained at the time of sale of the
extended warranty to the consumer.&#160; We
include shipping and handling charges to customers in revenue, which is
recognized in the period the consumer purchases and pays for delivery.&#160; Shipping and handling costs that we incur are
included in cost of revenues.<b>&#160; </b>The application of our revenue
recognition policy does not involve significant uncertainties and is not
subject to accounting estimates or assumptions having significant sensitivity
to change.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Product
Warranty</font></i>.</b>&#160; We
provide a warranty for the replacement or repair of certain defective
units.&#160; Our standard warranty policy
requires us to repair or replace certain defective units at no cost to our
customers.&#160; We estimate the costs that
may be incurred under our warranty and record an accrual in the amount of such
costs at the time we recognize product revenue.&#160;
Factors that affect our warranty accrual for covered units include the
number of units sold, historical and anticipated rates of warranty claims on
these units, and the cost of such claims.&#160;
We periodically assess the adequacy of our recorded warranty accrual and
adjust the amounts as necessary.<b>&#160; </b>Historically, our actual
experience has not differed significantly from our estimates.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">25</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='25',FILE='C:\JMS\sjayara\09-1423-1\task3432211\1423-1-ba-05.htm',USER='105898',CD='Mar 19 01:09 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of
Contents</a></font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Trade Receivables</font></i>.</b><b>&#160; </b>We carry trade receivables at the
original invoice amount less an estimate made for doubtful accounts based on a
monthly review of all outstanding amounts.&#160;
Management determines the allowance for doubtful accounts by regularly
evaluating individual customer receivables and considering a customer&#146;s
financial condition, credit history and current economic conditions.&#160; We write off trade receivables when we deem
them uncollectible.&#160; We record recoveries
of trade receivables previously written off when we receive them.&#160; We consider a trade receivable to be past due
if any portion of the receivable balance is outstanding for more than ninety
days.&#160; We do not charge interest on past
due receivables.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Inventories</font></i>.</b>&#160; Our inventories, consisting principally of
appliances, are stated at the lower of cost, determined on a specific
identification basis, or market.&#160; We
provide estimated provisions for the obsolescence of our appliance inventories,
including adjustments to market, based on various factors, including the age of
such inventory and our management&#146;s assessment of the need for such
provisions.&#160; We look at historical
inventory agings and margin analysis in determining our provision estimate.&#160; Historically, our actual experience has not
differed significantly from our estimates.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Income
Taxes</font></i>.</b><i>&#160; </i>We account for income taxes under
the liability method in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;109, <i>Accounting
for Income Taxes</i>.&#160; Deferred
tax liabilities are recognized for temporary differences that will result in
taxable amounts in future years.&#160;
Deferred tax assets are recognized for deductible temporary differences
and tax operating loss and tax credit carryforwards.&#160; Deferred tax assets and liabilities are measured
using the enacted tax rates expected to apply to taxable income in the periods
in which the deferred tax asset or liability is expected to be realized or
settled.&#160; We assess the likelihood that
our deferred tax assets will be recovered from future taxable income and record
a valuation allowance to reduce our deferred tax assets to the amounts we
believe to be realizable.&#160; We concluded
that a full valuation allowance against our U.S. deferred tax assets was appropriate
as of January&nbsp;3, 2009.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Share-Based
Compensation</font></i>.</b>&#160; We
account for share-based compensation in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;123
(revised 2004), <i>Share Based Payment</i>, using the
modified prospective method.&#160; Under this
method, we recognize compensation expense on a straight-line basis over the vesting
period for all share-based awards granted on or after January&nbsp;1, 2006, and
for previously granted awards not yet vested as of January&nbsp;1, 2006.&#160; We use the Black-Scholes option pricing model
to determine the fair value of awards at the grant date.&#160; We calculate the expected volatility for
stock options and awards using historical volatility.&#160; We estimate a 0%-5% forfeiture rate for stock
options issued to all employees and Board of Directors members, but will continue
to review these estimates in future periods.&#160;
The risk-free rates for the expected terms of the stock options are
based on the U.S. Treasury yield curve in effect at the time of the grant.&#160; The expected life represents the period that
the stock option awards are expected to be outstanding.&#160; The expected dividend yield is zero as we
have not paid or declared any cash dividends on our Common Stock.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:-.15pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">RECENTLY ISSUED ACCOUNTING PRONOUNCEMENTS</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:-.15pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In May&nbsp;2008, the
Financial Accounting Standards Board (&#147;FASB&#148;) issued Statement of Financial
Accounting Standards (&#147;SFAS&#148;) No.&nbsp;162, <i>The
Hierarchy of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles</i>.&#160; This standard is intended to improve
financial reporting by identifying a consistent framework, or hierarchy, for
selecting accounting principles to be used in preparing financial statements
that are presented in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles
in the United States for non-governmental entities.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;162 is effective 60 days
following approval by the SEC of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board&#146;s
amendments to AU Section&nbsp;411, <i>The
Meaning of</i>  <i>Present Fairly in
Conformity with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles</i>.&#160; We do not expect SFAS No.&nbsp;162 to have a
material impact on the preparation of our consolidated financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2008, the
FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;161, <i>Disclosures about
Derivative Instruments and Hedging Activities</i>.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;161 is intended to improve
financial reporting about derivative instruments and hedging activities by
requiring enhanced disclosures to enable investors to better understand their
effects on an entity&#146;s financial position, financial performance and cash
flows.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;161 also improves
transparency about the location and amounts of derivative instruments in an
entity&#146;s financial statements; how derivative instruments and related hedged
items are accounted for under SFAS No.&nbsp;133; and how derivative instruments
and related hedged items affect its financial position, financial performance
and cash flows.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;161 is
effective for financial statements issued for fiscal years and interim periods
beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2008, with early application encouraged.&#160; We do not believe the adoption of SFAS No.&nbsp;161
will have a material effect on our results of operations or financial position.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">26</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;160, <i>Noncontrolling Interests
in Consolidated Financial Statements, an amendment of ARB No.&nbsp;51</i>.&#160; This statement establishes accounting and
reporting standards for noncontrolling interests in subsidiaries and for the
deconsolidation of subsidiaries and clarifies that a noncontrolling interest in
a subsidiary is an ownership interest in the consolidated entity that should be
reported as equity in the consolidated financial statements.&#160; This statement also requires expanded
disclosures that clearly identify and distinguish between the interests of the
parent owners and the interests of the noncontrolling owners of a subsidiary.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;160 is effective for fiscal
years beginning on or after December&nbsp;15, 2008.&#160; We do not believe the adoption of SFAS No.&nbsp;160
will have a material effect on our results of operations or financial position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;141 (Revised 2007), <i>Business
Combinations</i>.&#160; While this
statement retains the fundamental requirement of SFAS No.&nbsp;141 that the
acquisition method of accounting (which SFAS No.&nbsp;141 called the purchase
method) be used for all business combinations, SFAS No.&nbsp;141 (Revised 2007)
now establishes the principles and requirements for how an acquirer in a
business combination: recognizes and measures in its financial statements, the
identifiable assets acquired, the liabilities assumed, and any noncontrolling
interests in the acquiree; recognizes and measures the goodwill acquired in the
business combination or the gain from a bargain purchase; and determines what
information should be disclosed in the financial statements to enable users of
the financial statements to evaluate the nature and financial effects of the
business combination.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;141
(Revised 2007) is effective for fiscal years beginning on or after December&nbsp;15,
2008.&#160; We do not believe the adoption of
SFAS No.&nbsp;141 will have a material effect on our results of operations or
financial position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In February&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;159, <i>The Fair Value Option for
Financial Assets and Financial Liabilities</i>.&#160; This statement permits entities to choose to
measure many financial instruments and certain other items at fair value.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;159 is effective for fiscal
years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2007.&#160;
The adoption of SFAS No.&nbsp;159 on December&nbsp;30, 2007 had no
impact on our consolidated financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In September&nbsp;2006,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;157, <i>Fair Value Measurements</i>.&#160; This statement defines fair value,
establishes a framework for measuring fair value under generally accepted
accounting principles and expands disclosures about fair value measurements.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;157 is effective for financial
statements issued for fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2007;
however, during February&nbsp;2008, the FASB issued FASB Staff Position (&#147;FSP&#148;)
No.&nbsp;157-2 which deferred the effective date of certain provisions of SFAS No.&nbsp;157
until fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2008.&#160; We adopted the provisions of SFAS No.&nbsp;157
that were not deferred under FSP No.&nbsp;157-2 effective December&nbsp;30,
2007.&#160; The adoption had no impact on our
consolidated financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Statements contained in
this annual report regarding our future operations, performance and results,
and anticipated liquidity discussed herein are forward-looking and, therefore,
are subject to certain risks and uncertainties, including, but not limited to,
those discussed herein.&#160; Any
forward-looking information regarding our operations will be affected primarily
by the speed at which individual retail outlets reach profitability, the volume
of appliance retail sales and the strength of energy conservation recycling
programs.&#160; Any forward-looking
information will also be affected by our continued ability to purchase product
from our suppliers at acceptable prices, the ability of individual retail
stores to meet planned revenue levels, the rate of growth in the number of
retail stores, costs and expenses being realized at higher than expected
levels, our ability to secure an adequate supply of special-buy appliances for
resale, the ability to secure appliance recycling contracts with sponsors of
energy efficiency programs, the ability of customers to supply units under
their recycling contracts with us, and the continued availability of our
current line of credit.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 7A.<a name="Item7a__235305"></a>&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; QUANTITATIVE AND QUALITATIVE
DISCLOSURES ABOUT MARKET RISK</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:-.15pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">Market Risk and Impact of Inflation</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="font-weight:bold;letter-spacing:-.15pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Interest
Rate Risk</font></i>.</b>&#160; We do
not believe there is any significant risk related to interest rate fluctuations
on our long-term fixed-rate debt.&#160; There
is interest rate risk on the line of credit, since our interest rate floats
with prime.&#160; The outstanding balance on </p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">27</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">our line of credit
as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 was approximately $14.5 million.&#160; There is also interest rate risk on
approximately $2.7 million in long-term debt entered into in September&nbsp;2002,
since our interest rate is based on the 30-day LIBOR rate.&#160; Although the $14.5 million line of credit is
subject to a minimum interest rate of 6.25%, based on average floating rate
borrowings of $17.2 million, a hypothetical 100 basis point change in the
applicable interest rate would have caused our interest expense to change by
approximately $0.2 million.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Foreign Currency Exchange Rate Risk.</font></i></b>&nbsp;&nbsp;We
currently generate revenues in Canada.&#160;
The reporting currency for our consolidated financial statements is
U.S.&nbsp;dollars.&#160; It is not possible to
determine the exact impact of foreign currency exchange rate changes; however,
the effect on reported revenue and net earnings can be estimated.&nbsp; We
estimate that the overall strength of the U.S. dollar against the Canadian
dollar had an unfavorable impact on revenue of approximately $0.6 million in
fiscal 2008.&nbsp; In addition, we estimate that such strength had an unfavorable
impact of approximately $0.2 million on our net income in the fiscal year
2008.&#160; We do not currently hedge foreign
currency fluctuations and do not intend to do so for the foreseeable future.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We do not hold any
derivative financial instruments nor do we hold any securities for trading or
speculative purposes.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Also, we believe
the decline in the housing and credit markets could adversely affect buying
habits of our customers in 2009.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 8.<a name="Item8__235322"></a>&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND
SUPPLEMENTARY DATA</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

 <tr>
  <td width="92%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:92.12%;">

<div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">REPORT OF INDEPENDENT REGISTERED PUBLIC ACCOUNTING
FIRM<a name="ReportOfIndependentRegisteredPubl_235508"></a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To the Shareholders,
Audit Committee and Board of Directors</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Appliance Recycling
Centers of America,&nbsp;Inc. and Subsidiaries</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Minneapolis, Minnesota</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have audited the
accompanying consolidated balance sheets of Appliance Recycling Centers of
America,&nbsp;Inc. and Subsidiaries (the Company) as of January&nbsp;3, 2009
and December&nbsp;29, 2007, and the related consolidated statements of
operations, shareholders&#146; equity and comprehensive income (loss) and cash flows
for the fiscal years then ended.&#160; These
consolidated financial statements are the responsibility of the company&#146;s management.&#160; Our responsibility is to express an opinion
on these consolidated financial statements based on our audits.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We conducted our audits
in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight
Board (United States).&#160; Those standards
require that we plan and perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about
whether the consolidated financial statements are free of material
misstatement.&#160; The company is not
required to have, nor were we engaged to perform, an audit of its internal
control over financial reporting. Our audits included consideration of its
internal control over financial reporting as a basis for designing audit
procedures that are appropriate in the circumstances, but not for the purpose
of expressing an opinion on the effectiveness of the company&#146;s internal control
over financial reporting.&#160; Accordingly,
we express no such opinion.&#160; An audit
includes examining, on a test basis, evidence supporting the amounts and
disclosures in the consolidated financial statements.&#160; An audit also includes assessing the
accounting principles used and significant estimates made by management as well
as evaluating the overall consolidated financial statement presentation.&#160; We believe that our audits provide a
reasonable basis for our opinion.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In our opinion, the
consolidated financial statements referred to above present fairly, in all
material respects, the financial position of Appliance Recycling Centers of
America,&nbsp;Inc. and Subsidiaries as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007 and the results of their operations and cash flows for the fiscal years
then ended, in conformity with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">/s/ Virchow, Krause&nbsp;&amp;
Company, LLP</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Minneapolis, MN</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">March&nbsp;19, 2009</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">29</font></p>

  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.12%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.88%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="70%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:70.0%;">

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">APPLIANCE
RECYCLING CENTERS OF AMERICA, INC.</font></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">NOTES TO
CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS</font></b><a name="NotesToConsolidatedFinancialState_002639"></a></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">(In
Thousands, Except Per Share Amounts)</font></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">1.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Nature of Business
and Basis of Presentation</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Nature of
business</font></u>:</i><b>&#160; </b>Appliance Recycling Centers of
America,&nbsp;Inc. and Subsidiaries (&#147;we,&#148; the &#147;Company&#148; or &#147;ARCA&#148;) are in the
business of selling new major household appliances through a chain of
Company-owned factory outlet stores under the name ApplianceSmart<font size="1" style="font-size:6.5pt;position:relative;top:-3.0pt;">&#174;</font>.&#160;
We also provide turnkey appliance recycling services for electric
utilities and other sponsors of energy efficiency programs.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Principles
of consolidation</font></u>:</i>&#160;
The consolidated financial statements include the accounts of Appliance
Recycling Centers of America,&nbsp;Inc. and our subsidiaries.&#160; All significant intercompany accounts and
transactions have been eliminated in consolidation.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We were a sixty
percent owner in North America Appliance Company, LLC (&#147;NAACO&#148;).&#160; NAACO was formed and commenced operations in June&nbsp;2003
and was a retailer of special-buy appliances in Texas.&#160; The operating results of NAACO have been
consolidated in our financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We were a sixty
percent owner in Productos Duraderos de Norte America (&#147;PDN&#148;), a Mexican
corporation.&#160; PDN was acquired in September&nbsp;2006
and refurbished room air conditioners for sale through our NAACO operation in
McAllen, Texas, and through our ApplianceSmart Factory Outlet stores.&#160; The operating results of PDN have been
consolidated in our financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the fourth
quarter of 2008, we planned and executed the shutdown of our NAACO and PDN businesses.&#160; NAACO and PDN were not operating as planned
and were no longer economically viable.&#160;
See Note 4 of &#147;Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements&#148; for more
detail.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ARCA Canada Inc.,
a Canadian corporation, is a wholly owned subsidiary.&#160; ARCA Canada was formed in September&nbsp;2006
to provide turnkey recycling services for electric utility energy efficiency
programs.&#160; The operating results of ARCA
Canada have been consolidated in our financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Reclassifications</font></u>:</i>&#160; Certain prior year items have been
reclassified to conform to current year presentation.&#160; We reclassified interest income from other
income (expense) to offset interest expense in the consolidated statements of
operations.&#160; We reclassified the results
of our discontinued operations below income from continuing operations in the
consolidated statements of operations.&#160;
In Note 12 to &#147;Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements,&#148; we
reclassified certain assets between our retail, recycling and unallocated
corporate segments.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Fair
value of financial instruments</font></u>:</i><b>  </b>&#160;The following methods and assumptions are used
to estimate the fair value of each class of financial instrument:</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 27.35pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Cash and
cash equivalents, accounts receivable and accounts payable:</font></i>&#160; Due to their nature and short-term maturities,
the carrying amounts approximate fair value.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 27.0pt;text-indent:-27.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 27.0pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Short-
and long-term debt:&#160; </font></i>The
fair value of short- and long-term debt approximates carrying value and has
been estimated based on discounted cash flows using interest rates being
offered for similar debt having the same or similar remaining maturities and
collateral requirements.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 27.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 27.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">No separate
comparison of fair values versus carrying values is presented for the
aforementioned financial instruments since their fair values are not
significantly different than their balance sheet carrying amounts.&#160; In addition, the aggregate fair values of the
financial instruments would not represent the underlying value of our Company.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">34</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
