<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='34',FILE='C:\JMS\sjayara\09-1423-1\task3432211\1423-1-ba-09.htm',USER='105898',CD='Mar 19 01:14 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Estimates</font></u>:</i>&#160; The preparation of financial statements in
conformity with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States
of America requires our management to make estimates and assumptions that
affect the reported amounts of assets and liabilities and disclosure of
contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the financial statements and
the reported amounts of revenues and expenses during the reporting period.&#160; Significant items subject to estimates and
assumptions include the valuation allowances for accounts receivable,
inventories and deferred tax assets, accrued expenses, and the assumptions we
use to value stock-based compensation.&#160;
Actual results could differ from those estimates.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Fiscal
year</font></u>:</i>&#160; We report on
a 52- or 53-week fiscal year.&#160; Our 2008
fiscal year (&#147;2008&#148;) ended on January&nbsp;3, 2009 and included 53 weeks.&#160; Our 2007 fiscal year (&#147;2007&#148;) ended on December&nbsp;29,
2007 and included 52 weeks.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Recent
Accounting Pronouncements</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In May&nbsp;2008, the
Financial Accounting Standards Board (&#147;FASB&#148;) issued Statement of Financial
Accounting Standards (&#147;SFAS&#148;) No.&nbsp;162, <i>The
Hierarchy of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles</i>.&#160; This standard is intended to improve
financial reporting by identifying a consistent framework, or hierarchy, for
selecting accounting principles to be used in preparing financial statements
that are presented in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles
in the United States for non-governmental entities.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;162 is effective 60 days
following approval by the SEC of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board&#146;s
amendments to AU Section&nbsp;411, <i>The
Meaning of</i>  <i>Present Fairly in
Conformity with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles</i>.&#160; We do not expect SFAS No.&nbsp;162 to have a
material impact on the preparation of our consolidated financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2008, the
FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;161, <i>Disclosures about
Derivative Instruments and Hedging Activities</i>.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;161 is intended to improve
financial reporting about derivative instruments and hedging activities by
requiring enhanced disclosures to enable investors to better understand their
effects on an entity&#146;s financial position, financial performance and cash
flows.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;161 also improves transparency
about the location and amounts of derivative instruments in an entity&#146;s
financial statements; how derivative instruments and related hedged items are
accounted for under SFAS No.&nbsp;133; and how derivative instruments and
related hedged items affect its financial position, financial performance and
cash flows.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;161 is
effective for financial statements issued for fiscal years and interim periods
beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2008, with early application encouraged.&#160; We do not believe the adoption of SFAS No.&nbsp;161
will have a material effect on our results of operations or financial position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;160, <i>Noncontrolling Interests
in Consolidated Financial Statements, an amendment of ARB No.&nbsp;51</i>.&#160; This statement establishes accounting and
reporting standards for noncontrolling interests in subsidiaries and for the
deconsolidation of subsidiaries and clarifies that a noncontrolling interest in
a subsidiary is an ownership interest in the consolidated entity that should be
reported as equity in the consolidated financial statements.&#160; This statement also requires expanded
disclosures that clearly identify and distinguish between the interests of the
parent owners and the interests of the noncontrolling owners of a subsidiary.&#160; SFAS No.160 is effective for fiscal years
beginning on or after December&nbsp;15, 2008.&#160;
We do not believe the adoption of SFAS No.&nbsp;160 will have a material
effect on our results of operations or financial position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;141 (Revised 2007), <i>Business
Combinations</i>.&#160; While this
statement retains the fundamental requirement of SFAS No.&nbsp;141 that the
acquisition method of accounting (which SFAS No.&nbsp;141 called the purchase
method) be used for all business combinations, SFAS No.&nbsp;141 (Revised 2007)
now establishes the principles and requirements for how an acquirer in a
business combination: recognizes and measures in its financial statements, the
identifiable assets acquired, the liabilities assumed, and any noncontrolling
interests in the acquiree; recognizes and measures the goodwill acquired in the
business combination or the gain from a bargain purchase; and determines what
information should be disclosed in the financial statements to enable users of
the financial statements to evaluate the nature and financial effects of the
business combination.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;141
(Revised 2007) is effective for fiscal years beginning on or after December&nbsp;15,
2008.&#160; We do not believe the adoption of
SFAS No.&nbsp;141 will have a material effect on our results of operations or
financial position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In February&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;159, <i>The Fair Value Option for
Financial Assets and Financial Liabilities</i>.&#160; This statement permits entities to choose to
measure many financial instruments and certain other items at fair value.&#160; </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">35</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='35',FILE='C:\JMS\sjayara\09-1423-1\task3432211\1423-1-ba-09.htm',USER='105898',CD='Mar 19 01:14 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">SFAS No.&nbsp;159 is
effective for fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2007.&#160; The adoption of SFAS No.&nbsp;159 on December&nbsp;30,
2007 had no impact on our consolidated financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In September&nbsp;2006,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;157, <i>Fair Value Measurements</i>.&#160; This statement defines fair value,
establishes a framework for measuring fair value under generally accepted
accounting principles and expands disclosures about fair value
measurements.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;157 is
effective for financial statements issued for fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15,
2007; however, during February&nbsp;2008, the FASB issued FASB Staff Position (&#147;FSP&#148;)
No.&nbsp;157-2 which deferred the effective date of certain provisions of SFAS No.&nbsp;157
until fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2008.&#160; We adopted the provisions of SFAS No.&nbsp;157
that were not deferred under FSP No.&nbsp;157-2 effective December&nbsp;30,
2007.&#160; The adoption had no impact on our
consolidated financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">3.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Significant
Accounting Policies</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Cash and cash equivalents</font></u>:</i>&#160; We consider all highly liquid investments
purchased with original maturity dates of three months or less to be cash
equivalents.&#160; We maintain our cash in
bank deposit and money-market accounts which, at times, exceed federally
insured limits.&#160; We have not experienced
any losses in such accounts.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Trade receivables</font></u>:</i><b>&#160; </b>We carry trade receivables at the
original invoice amount less an estimate made for doubtful accounts based on a
monthly review of all outstanding amounts.&#160;
Management determines the allowance for doubtful accounts by regularly evaluating
individual customer receivables and considering a customer&#146;s financial
condition, credit history and current economic conditions.&#160; We write off trade receivables when we deem
them uncollectible.&#160; We record recoveries
of trade receivables previously written off when we receive them.&#160; We consider a trade receivable to be past due
if any portion of the receivable balance is outstanding for more than ninety
days.&#160; We do not charge interest on past
due receivables.&#160; Our management
considers the allowance for doubtful accounts of $292 and $152 to be adequate
to cover any exposure to loss as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007, respectively.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Inventories</font></u>:</i>&#160;
Inventories, consisting principally of appliances, are stated at the
lower of cost, determined on a specific identification basis, or market and
consist of:</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We provide
estimated provisions for the obsolescence of our appliance inventories,
including adjustments to market, based on various factors, including the age of
such inventory and our management&#146;s assessment of the need for such
provisions.&#160; We look at historical
inventory agings and margin analysis in determining our provision estimate.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Property
and equipment</font></u>:</i>&#160; Property and equipment are stated at
cost.&#160; We compute depreciation using
straight-line and accelerated methods over the following estimated useful
lives:</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We amortize leasehold
improvements on a straight-line basis over the shorter of their estimated
useful lives or the underlying lease term.&#160;
Repair and maintenance costs are charged to operations as incurred.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property and equipment
consists of the following:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">January&nbsp;3,<br>
  2009</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">

  2007</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Depreciation
and amortization expense</font></u></i>:&#160;
Depreciation and amortization expense related to buildings and equipment
from our recycling centers is presented in cost of revenues and depreciation
and amortization expense related to buildings and equipment from our
ApplianceSmart Factory Outlet stores and corporate assets, such as furniture
and computers, is presented in selling, general and administrative expenses in
the consolidated statements of operations.&#160;
Depreciation and amortization expense was $1,123 and $873 for the fiscal
years ended January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007, respectively.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Software development costs</font></u>:</i>&#160; We capitalize
software developed for internal use in accordance with Statement of Position
98-1, <i>Accounting for the Costs of Computer Software
Developed or Obtained for Internal Use,</i> and are amortizing such
costs over their estimated useful lives of three to five years.&#160; Costs capitalized were $262 and $231 for the
years ended January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007, respectively.&#160; Amortization expense on software development
costs was $256 and $260 for the fiscal years 2008 and 2007, respectively.&#160; Estimated amortization expenses are $263,
$177, $71, $8, and $3 for the fiscal years 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012 and 2013,
respectively.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Impairment
of long-lived assets</font></u></i><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">:</font></i><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp; </font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We evaluate long-lived assets such as
property and equipment for impairment whenever events or changes in
circumstances indicate the carrying value of an asset may not be
recoverable.&#160; We assess impairment based
on the estimated future net undiscounted cash flows expected to result from the
use of the assets, including cash flows from disposition.&#160; Should the sum of the expected future net
cash flows be less than the carrying value, we recognize an impairment loss at
that time.&#160; We measure an impairment loss
by comparing the amount by which the carrying value exceeds the fair value
(estimated discounted future cash flows or appraisal of assets) of the
long-lived assets.&#160; We recognized no
impairment charges during the fiscal years ended January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Deferred financing fees</font></u>:</i>&#160; Deferred financing fees are presented in the
consolidated balance sheets as a component of other assets and are reported net
of accumulated amortization.&#160; We record
amortization expense on a straight-line basis over the term of the underlying
debt.&#160; Deferred financing fees, net of
accumulated amortization, were $38 and $51 as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007, respectively.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Accounting for leases</font></u></i>:&#160; We conduct the majority of our retail and
recycling operations from leased facilities.&#160;
The majority of our leases require payment of real estate taxes,
insurance and common area maintenance in addition to rent.&#160; The terms of our lease agreements typically
range from five to ten years.&#160; Most of
the leases contain renewal clauses and escalation clauses, and certain store
leases require contingent rents based on factors such as revenue.&#160; For leases that contain predetermined fixed
escalations of the minimum rent, we recognize the related rent expense on a
straight-line basis from the date we take possession of the property to the end
of the initial lease term.&#160; We record any
difference between straight-line rent amounts and amounts payable under the
leases as part of deferred rent in accrued expenses.&#160; Cash or lease incentives (tenant allowances)
received upon entering into certain store leases are recognized on a
straight-line basis as a reduction to rent from the date we take possession of
the property through the end of the initial lease term.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Product warranty</font></u>:</i>&#160; We provide a warranty for the replacement or
repair of certain defective units.&#160; Our
standard warranty policy requires us to repair or replace certain defective
units at no cost to our customers.&#160; We
estimate the costs that may be incurred under our warranty and record an
accrual in the amount of such costs at the time we recognize product
revenue.&#160; Factors that affect our
warranty accrual for covered units include the number of units sold, historical
and anticipated rates of </p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">37</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">warranty claims on these
units, and the cost of such claims.&#160; We
periodically assess the adequacy of our recorded warranty accrual and adjust
the amounts as necessary.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Changes in our warranty
accrual for the fiscal years ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007 are as follows:</font></p>

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Income taxes</font></u>:&#160; </i>We account for income taxes under the
liability method in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;109, <i>Accounting
for Income Taxes</i>.&#160; Deferred
tax liabilities are recognized for temporary differences that will result in
taxable amounts in future years.&#160;
Deferred tax assets are recognized for deductible temporary differences
and tax operating loss and tax credit carryforwards.&#160; Deferred tax assets and liabilities are
measured using the enacted tax rates expected to apply to taxable income in the
periods in which the deferred tax asset or liability is expected to be realized
or settled.&#160; We assess the likelihood
that our deferred tax assets will be recovered from future taxable income and
record a valuation allowance to reduce our deferred tax assets to the amounts
we believe to be realizable.&#160; We
concluded that a full valuation allowance against our U.S. deferred tax assets
was appropriate as of January&nbsp;3, 2009.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Share-based
compensation</font></u>:</i>&#160; We
account for share-based compensation in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;123
(revised 2004), <i>Share Based Payment</i>, using the
modified prospective method.&#160; Under this
method, we recognize compensation expense on a straight-line basis over the
vesting period for all share-based awards granted.&#160; We use the Black-Scholes option pricing model
to determine the fair value of awards at the grant date.&#160; We calculate the expected volatility for
stock options and awards using historical volatility.&#160; We estimate a 0%-5% forfeiture rate for stock
options issued to all employees and Board of Directors members, but will
continue to review these estimates in future periods.&#160; The risk-free rates for the expected terms of
the stock options are based on the U.S. Treasury yield curve in effect at the
time of the grant.&#160; The expected life
represents the period that the stock option awards are expected to be
outstanding.&#160; The expected dividend yield
is zero as we have not paid or declared any cash dividends on our Common
Stock.&#160; Based on these valuations, we
recognized share-based compensation expense of $529 and $107 for the fiscal
years ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007,
respectively.&#160; We estimate that the
expense for fiscal 2009 will be approximately $527, based on the value of
options outstanding as of January&nbsp;3, 2009.&#160;
This estimate does not include any expense for additional options that
may be granted and vest during 2009.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Comprehensive
income</font></u>:</i>&#160; We report
comprehensive income or loss in accordance with the provisions of SFAS&nbsp;No.&nbsp;130,
<i>Reporting Comprehensive Income</i>.&#160; SFAS&nbsp;No.&nbsp;130 establishes standards
for the reporting and display of comprehensive income and its components.&#160; Other comprehensive income refers to
revenues, expenses, gains and losses that under generally accepted accounting principles
are included in comprehensive income but are excluded from net income as these
amounts are recorded directly as an adjustment to shareholders&#146; equity.&#160; Our other comprehensive income is comprised
of foreign currency translation adjustments.&#160;
The effect of the foreign currency translation adjustments, net of tax,
was a loss of $452 for the fiscal year ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and income
of $76 for the fiscal year ending December&nbsp;29, 2007.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Revenue
recognition</font></u>:</i>&#160; We
recognize revenue from appliance sales in the period the consumer purchases and
pays for the appliance, net of an allowance for estimated returns.&#160; We recognize revenue from appliance recycling
when we collect and process a unit.&#160; We
recognize byproduct revenue upon shipment.&#160;
We recognize revenue on extended warranties with retained service
obligations on a straight-line basis over the period of the warranty in
accordance with FASB Technical Bulletin 90-1, <i>Accounting
for Separately Priced Extended Warranty and Product Maintenance Contracts</i>.&#160; On extended warranty arrangements that we
sell but others service for a fixed portion of the warranty sales price, we
recognize revenue for the net amount retained at the time of sale of the
extended warranty to the consumer.&#160; We
include shipping and handling charges to </p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">38</font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">customers in revenue, which is recognized in the period the consumer
purchases and pays for the delivery.&#160;
Shipping and handling costs that we incur are included in cost of
revenues.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Taxes
collected from customers</font></u>:</i><b>&#160; </b>We account for taxes collected
from customers on a net basis in accordance with Emerging Issues Task Force
Issue No.&nbsp;06-3, <i>How Taxes Collected from
Customers and Remitted to Governmental Authorities Should Be Presented in the
Income Statement (That Is, Gross versus Net Presentation)</i>.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Advertising
expense</font></u>:</i><b>  </b>&#160;Our policy is to expense advertising costs as
incurred.&#160; Advertising expense was $4,258
and $4,447 for the fiscal years ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007, respectively.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Basic and
diluted net income (loss) per share</font></u>:</i>&#160; We calculate net income (loss) per share in
accordance with SFAS&nbsp;No.&nbsp;128, <i>Earnings
Per Share</i> (SFAS&nbsp;128).&#160;
Basic earnings per share is computed based on the weighted average
number of common shares outstanding.&#160;
Diluted earnings per share is computed based on the weighted average
number of common shares outstanding adjusted by the number of additional shares
that would have been outstanding had the potentially dilutive common shares
been issued.&#160; Potentially dilutive shares
of Common Stock include unexercised stock options.&#160; Basic per share amounts are computed,
generally, by dividing net income (loss) by the weighted average number of
common shares outstanding.&#160; Diluted per
share amounts assume the conversion, exercise or issuance of all potential
Common Stock instruments unless their effect is antidilutive, thereby reducing
the loss or increasing the income per common share.&#160; In calculating diluted weighted average
shares and per share amounts, we included stock options (see Note 10) with
exercise prices below average market prices, for the respective fiscal years in
which they were dilutive, using the treasury stock method.&#160; We calculated the number of additional shares
by assuming the outstanding stock options were exercised and that the proceeds
from such exercises were used to acquire Common Stock at the average market
price during the year.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3,
2009, we excluded 303 options from the diluted weighted average share
outstanding calculation as the effect of these options is anti-dilutive.&#160; The effect of all options outstanding in
fiscal year 2007 was dilutive.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">A reconciliation
of the denominator in the basic and diluted income or loss per share is as
follows:</font></p>

  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Numerator:</font></b></p>

<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='39',FILE='C:\JMS\sjayara\09-1423-1\task3432211\1423-1-ba-09.htm',USER='105898',CD='Mar 19 01:14 2009' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">4.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Discontinued
Operations</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the fourth
quarter of 2008, we planned and executed the shutdown of our NAACO and PDN
operations.&#160; NAACO and PDN were not
operating as planned and were no longer economically viable.&#160; In 2008, our supply of room air conditioners
from a major manufacturer was depleted, no longer providing refurbishment
opportunities for PDN and revenues for NAACO, which was the basis for our
investment in these businesses.&#160; We will
not have any continuing involvement or significant continuing cash flows in
these businesses.&#160; The results of
operations for NAACO and PDN were included in our retail segment.&#160; During the fiscal years ended January&nbsp;3,
2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007, NAACO and PDN had revenues of $792 and $1,011,
respectively, and income (loss) before taxes of ($882) and $63,
respectively.&#160; All results of operations
for periods presented prior to the abandonment date have been reclassified as
discontinued operations.&#160; We recorded a
loss on the disposal of NAACO and PDN of $692, net of tax, for the year ended January&nbsp;3,
2009, which consisted of termination costs and the write-off of certain assets
to fair value upon the abandonment of the operations in December&nbsp;2008.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">5.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Line of Credit</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have an $18,000
line of credit with a lender.&#160; The line
was increased from $16,000 to $18,000 on February&nbsp;5, 2008.&#160; The interest rate on the line as of January&nbsp;3,
2009 was 6.25% (the greater of prime plus 1.50 percentage points or 6.25%)
compared to 9.25% as of December&nbsp;29, 2007.&#160;
The weighted average interest rate for fiscal years 2008 and 2007 was
6.83% and 10.58%, respectively.&#160; The
amount of borrowings available under the line of credit is based on a formula
using receivables and inventories.&#160; Our
unused borrowing capacity under this line was $384 as of January&nbsp;3,
2009.&#160; We may not have access to the full
$18.0 million line of credit due to the formula using our receivables and
inventories.&#160; The line of credit has a
stated maturity date of December&nbsp;31, 2010, if not renewed, and provides
that the lender may demand payment in full of the entire outstanding balance of
the loan at any time.&#160; The line of credit
is collateralized by substantially all our assets and requires minimum monthly
interest payments of $58, regardless of the outstanding principal balance.&#160; The lender is also secured by an inventory
repurchase agreement with Whirlpool Corporation for purchases from Whirlpool
only.&#160; The loan requires that we meet
certain financial covenants, provides payment penalties for noncompliance and
prepayment, limits the amount of other debt we can incur, limits the amount of
spending on fixed assets and prohibits payments of dividends.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3, 2009, we were not in
compliance with certain financial covenants of the loan agreement and received
a waiver from the lender.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">6.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Long-Term Debt
Obligations</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Long-term debt and
capital lease obligations consisted of the following:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="97%" style="border-collapse:collapse;margin-left:.1in;width:97.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="74%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:74.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2008</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="10%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2007</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="74%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:74.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Adjustable rate

  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">

  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" style="border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.84%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-align:right;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5,471</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Capital leases and other financing obligations</font></u></i><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">:</font></i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We acquire certain equipment under capital
leases and other financing obligations.&#160;
The cost of the equipment was approximately $1,396 and $966 at January&nbsp;3,
2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007, respectively.&#160;
Accumulated amortization at January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007 was approximately $387 and $256, respectively.&#160; Depreciation and amortization expense for
2008 and 2007 is included in cost of revenue and selling, general and
administrative expenses.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In
September&nbsp;2008, we entered into a master equipment lease with a lender
providing up to $250 in available funds.&#160;
As of January&nbsp;3, 2009, we had utilized the entire lease line to
fund equipment for the new retail outlets opened in December&nbsp;2008 and January&nbsp;2009.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

schedule by fiscal year of the approximate remaining minimum payments required
under the leases and other financing obligations, together with the present
value at January&nbsp;3, 2009:</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">8.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Commitments and
Contingencies</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Operating leases</font></u>:</i><b>&#160; </b>We lease the
majority of our retail stores and recycling centers under noncancelable
operating leases.&#160; The leases typically
require the payment of taxes, maintenance, utilities and insurance.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-align:right;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">24,051</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Rent expense for the
years ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007 was $4,710 and
$4,069, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Contracts</font></u>:</i>&#160;
We have entered into contracts with three appliance manufacturers.&#160; Under the agreements there are no minimum
purchase commitments; however, we have agreed to indemnify the manufacturers
for certain claims, allegations or losses with respect to appliances we sell.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Litigation</font></u>:</i><b>&nbsp; </b>In December&nbsp;2004, we filed suit
in the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California alleging that
JACO Environmental,&nbsp;Inc. (JACO) and one of our former consultants
fraudulently obtained U.S. Patent No.&nbsp;6,732,416 in May&nbsp;2004 covering
appliance recycling methods and systems which were originally developed by us
beginning in 1987 and used in serving more than forty-five electric utility
appliance recycling programs up to the time the suit was filed.&nbsp; We sought
an injunction to prevent JACO from claiming that it obtained a valid patent on
appliance recycling processes that we believe is based on methods and processes
we invented.&#160; In addition, we asked the
Court to find that the patent obtained by JACO is unenforceable due to
inequitable conduct before the United States Patent Office.&nbsp; We also asked
the court for unspecified damages related to charges that JACO, in using the
patent to promote its services, engaged in unfair competition and false and
misleading advertising under federal and California statutes.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In September&nbsp;2005,
we received a legally binding document in which JACO stated it would not sue us
or any of our customers for violating the JACO patent.&#160; Further, the defendants in the case did not
assert any counterclaims against ARCA.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In January&nbsp;2009, the
Court granted JACO a summary judgment in ARCA&#146;s lawsuit against the
parties.&#160; The ruling was made by the same
judge who had earlier denied summary judgment to the defendants.&#160; Even though the Court&#146;s ruling will have no
impact on our method of recycling or ability to conduct existing or future
business, we filed an appeal with the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals in
California in February&nbsp;2009 seeking to have the court set aside the
summary judgment.&#160; We believe the
decision by the trial judge was in error and contrary to the law relating to
unfair competition and false advertising.&#160;
We believe we are entitled to our day in court against JACO for damages
caused by their actions, and we expect that a decision on the appeal will be
handed down late in 2009 or early in 2010.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On October&nbsp;24, 2006,
JACO and SEG Umwelt-Service/Basis of Mettlach, Germany (SEG) filed a patent
infringement lawsuit in Federal Court in San Francisco against us.&#160; The suit claimed that we had been using
refrigerator recycling systems and processes covered by two U.S. patents issued
to SEG and exclusively licensed to JACO.&nbsp; JACO and SEG sought an
undisclosed amount in damages, in addition to an injunction barring us from
continuing to use and market the systems and processes upon which we allegedly
infringed.&#160; This suit was subsequently
dismissed following a transfer of the case to Los Angeles upon the motion of
ARCA.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">42</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>

provision for income taxes with the federal statutory tax rate is shown below:</font></p>

  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Income tax
  expense at statutory rate</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">At January&nbsp;3, 2009,
we had a full valuation allowance against our U.S. deferred tax assets to
reduce the total to an amount our management believes is appropriate.&#160; Realization of deferred tax assets is
dependent upon sufficient future taxable income during the periods when
deductible temporary differences and carryforwards are expected to be available
to reduce taxable income.&#160; The valuation allowance
decreased in the current year primarily as a result of taxable income generated
during the year from U.S. sources.&#160; In
the future, when we believe we can reasonably estimate future operating results
and those estimated results reflect taxable income, the amount of deferred tax
assets considered reasonable could be adjusted by a reduction of the valuation
allowance.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have not recognized a
deferred tax liability relating to cumulative undistributed earnings of
controlled foreign subsidiaries that are essentially permanent in duration. &#160;If some or all of the undistributed earnings
of the controlled foreign subsidiaries are remitted to us in the future, income
taxes, if any, after the application of foreign tax credits will be provided at
that time.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Future utilization of net
operating loss (&#147;NOL&#148;) and tax credit carryforwards is subject to certain
limitations under provisions of Section&nbsp;382 of the Internal Revenue
Code.&#160; This section relates to a 50
percent change in control over a three-year period.&#160; We believe that the issuance of Common Stock
during 1999 resulted in an &#147;ownership change&#148; under Section&nbsp;382.&#160; Accordingly, our ability to use NOL and tax
credit carryforwards generated prior to February&nbsp;1999 is limited to
approximately $56 per year.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">At January&nbsp;3, 2009,
we had federal NOL carryforwards of approximately $8,650 ($6,695 of which is
subject to IRC section 382 limitations) and credits for general business
credits of $13.&#160; In addition to general
business credits we also had alternative minimum tax credits carried forward of
approximately $299.&#160; We also had state
NOL carryforwards of $20,025 ($3,241 of which is subject to IRC section 382
limitations).&#160; The NOL carryforwards are
available to offset future taxable income or reduce taxes payable through
2027.&#160; These loss carryforwards will
begin expiring in 2016.&#160; The general
business credits will expire in 2013.&#160; We
previously wrote off NOLs related to IRC section 382 limits against the
valuation allowance.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">A portion of our net
operating loss carryforwards (approximately $930 of tax deductions) resulted
from the exercise of stock options.&#160; When
these loss carryforwards are realized, the corresponding change in valuation
allowance will be recorded as additional paid-in capital.&#160; We have adopted the &#147;with and without&#148;
approach to determine tax benefits under FAS 123R.&#160; Under this approach, windfall tax benefits
are used last to offset taxable income and a benefit is recorded in additional
paid-in capital only if an incremental benefit is provided after considering
all other tax attributes (including NOLs) presently available to us.&#160; This treatment results in a
re-characterization of the NOL carryforward and a difference in the NOL asset
between the financial statements and tax returns.&#160; The difference in the federal NOL deferred
tax asset is $316.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">At January&nbsp;3, 2009,
we had NOL carryforwards not subject to IRC section 382 limitations, expiring
as follows:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="97%" style="border-collapse:collapse;margin-left:.1in;width:97.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="86%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:86.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2025</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.14%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.86%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">66</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="86%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:86.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2026</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.14%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="8%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:8.84%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1,889</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We account for uncertain
tax positions in accordance with the provisions of FASB Interpretation 48 (&#147;FIN
48&#148;), <i>Accounting for Uncertainty in Income Taxes</i>.&#160; As required by FIN 48, which clarifies FASB No.&nbsp;109,
<i>Accounting for Income Taxes</i>, we
recognize the financial statement benefit of a tax position only after
determining that the relevant tax authority would more likely than not sustain
the position.&#160; For tax positions meeting
the more-likely-than-not threshold, the amount recognized in the financial
statements is the largest benefit that has a greater than 50 percent likelihood
of being realized upon ultimate settlement with the relevant tax
authority.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3, 2009,
we did not have any material uncertain tax positions.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">44</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">It is our practice to
recognize interest related to income tax matters as a component of interest
expense and penalties as a component of selling, general and administrative
expense.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3, 2009, we
had an immaterial amount of accrued interest and penalties.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are subject to income
taxes in the U.S. federal jurisdiction, foreign jurisdictions and various state
jurisdictions.&#160; Tax regulations from each
jurisdiction are subject to the interpretation of the related tax laws and regulations
and require significant judgment to apply.&#160;
With few exceptions, we are no longer subject to U.S. federal, foreign,
state or local income tax examinations by tax authorities for the years before
2005.&#160; We are not currently under
examination by any taxing jurisdiction.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We had no significant
unrecognized tax benefits as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 that would reasonably be
expected to affect our effective tax rate during the next twelve months.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">10.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Shareholders&#146; Equity</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Stock options</font></u></i>:&#160; Our 2006 Stock Option Plan (the &#147;2006 Plan&#148;)
permits the granting of incentive stock options meeting the requirements of Section&nbsp;422
of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended, and nonqualified options that
do not meet the requirements of Section&nbsp;422.&#160; The 2006 Plan has 600 shares available for
grant.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3, 2009, 300
options were outstanding to employees and non-employee directors and 15 options
have been exercised under the 2006 Plan.&#160;
Our Restated 1997 Stock Option Plan (the &#147;1997 Plan&#148;) has expired, but
the options outstanding under the expired 1997 Plan continue to be exercisable
in accordance with their terms.&#160; As of January&nbsp;3,
2009, options to purchase an aggregate of 39 shares were outstanding under the
1997 Plan.&#160; Options granted to employees
typically vest over two years while grants to non-employee directors vest in
six months.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The fair value of each
option grant is estimated on the date of grant using the Black-Scholes option
pricing model with the following weighted-average assumptions:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The weighted average fair
value per option of options granted during fiscal years 2008 and 2007 was $4.26
and $2.45, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The aggregate intrinsic
value in the preceding tables represent the total pre-tax intrinsic value,
based on our closing stock price of $3.07 on January&nbsp;3, 2009, which
theoretically could have been received by the option holders had all option
holders exercised their options as of that date.&#160; The total number of in-the-money options
exercisable as of January&nbsp;3, 2009 was 23.&#160;
The intrinsic value of stock options exercised during the fiscal years
ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29, 2007 was $291 and $797,
respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Preferred stock</font></u>:</i><b>&#160; </b>Our amended Articles of Incorporation
authorize two million shares of Preferred Stock that may be issued from time to
time in one or more series having such rights, powers, preferences and
designations as the Board of Directors may determine.&#160; To date no such preferred shares have been
issued.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">11.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Major Customers and
Suppliers</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For
the fiscal year ended January&nbsp;3, 2009, one customer represented 10% of our
total revenues and had outstanding receivables of $2,121.&#160; For the fiscal year ended December&nbsp;29,
2007, one customer represented 12% of our total revenues and had outstanding
receivables of $5,934.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the two fiscal
years ended January&nbsp;3, 2009, we purchased a vast majority of appliances
for resale from three suppliers.&#160; We have
and are continuing to secure other vendors from which to purchase
appliances.&#160; However, the curtailment or
loss of one of these suppliers or any appliance supplier could adversely affect
our operations.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">12.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Segment Information</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="background:white;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We operate within
targeted markets through two reportable segments: retail and recycling.&#160; The
retail operation is comprised of income generated through our ApplianceSmart
Factory Outlet stores.&#160; The recycling
operation is comprised of all fees charged and costs incurred for collecting,
recycling and installing appliances for utilities and other customers. &#160;The nature of products, services and customers
for both segments varies significantly.&#160;
As such, the segments are managed separately.&#160; Our Chief Executive Officer has been
identified as the Chief Operating Decision Maker (&#147;CODM&#148;).&#160; The CODM evaluates performance and allocates
resources based on sales and income from operations of each segment.&#160; Income from operations represents revenues
less cost of revenues and operating expenses, including certain allocated
selling, general and administrative costs. The accounting policies of the
reportable segments are the same as those described in Note 3, &#147;Significant
Accounting Policies.&#148;&#160; There are no
intersegment sales or transfers.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">46</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">13.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Benefit Contribution Plan</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have a defined
contribution salary deferral plan covering substantially all employees under Section&nbsp;401(k)&nbsp;of
the Internal Revenue Code.&#160; We contribute
an amount equal to 10 cents for each dollar contributed by each employee up to
a maximum of 5% of each employee&#146;s compensation.&#160; We recognized expense for contributions to
the plan of $30 and $26 for the fiscal years ending January&nbsp;3, 2009 and December&nbsp;29,
2007, respectively.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">47</font></p>
